name: Sudan (Republic of the Sudan / جمهورية السودان)
summary: Sudan is a Northeast African religious-law federal state operating under a primarily Hanafi-Sunni Sharia framework following the 1983 Sharia-based 'September Laws' reform and the 2020 transitional government's signalled (though partial) repeal/reform efforts. Family-law framework operates under the Muslim Personal Status Law 1991 (Qanun al-Ahwal al-Shakhsiyya li-l-Muslimin) drawing on Hanafi jurisprudence with some Maliki provisions, and the Personal Status Law for Non-Muslims 1926 for non-Muslim communities. Custody (hadana) and guardianship (wilaya) are governed by Muslim Personal Status Law arts. 110-134. The Supreme Court (Mahkamat al-Tamyiz) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court was suspended post-2019. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in Personal Status Courts. Psychology profession regulation operates through the Sudan Medical Council framework. Sudan is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. Sudan is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Muslim Personal Status Law — Custody and guardianship
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Muslim Personal Status Law drawing primarily on Hanafi jurisprudence with some Maliki provisions. Arts. 110-134 govern hadana (custody) and wilaya (guardianship).

statutory_framework.title: Personal Status Law for Non-Muslims
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Personal Status Law for non-Muslim communities — among the earliest colonial-era African personal-status statutes.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court (Mahkamat al-Tamyiz)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

professional_regulators.name: Sudan Medical Council
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of medical and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Sudanese family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Personal Status Law for Non-Muslims 1926 + Anglo-Egyptian framework
key_developments.description: Federal Personal Status Law for non-Muslims enacted under Anglo-Egyptian colonial administration 1899-1956 — among the earliest colonial-era African personal-status statutes within the corpus. Foundational pre-independence framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Sudan independence + Republic + Nimeiri-era framework
key_developments.description: Sudan independence 1 January 1956 from Anglo-Egyptian Condominium — substantively distinctive Northeast African post-colonial framework. Subsequent recurring military-coup pattern affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-modernisation.

key_developments.title: Nimeiri coup + 16-year-authoritarian framework
key_developments.description: Gaafar Nimeiri coup d'état 25 May 1969 — substantively distinctive Northeast African 16-year-authoritarian framework 1969-1985. Substantive transition from Marxist-Leninist (1969-1971) to Islamist (1977 reconciliation) framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: September Laws — Sharia reform
key_developments.description: Comprehensive Sharia-based legal reform 8 September 1983 under Nimeiri government — established Hanafi Sharia as primary framework. Substantively distinctive globally trigger of Second Sudanese Civil War (1983-2005) due to imposition on Christian/animist South. Foundational pre-1991-Muslim-Personal-Status-Law framework.

key_developments.title: Bashir coup + National Islamic Front + 30-year-Islamist-regime framework
key_developments.description: Omar al-Bashir coup d'état 30 June 1989 ending Sadiq al-Mahdi government — substantively distinctive Northeast African 30-year-Islamist-regime framework 1989-2019. Substantive National Islamic Front + National Congress Party framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Sudan ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Sudan ratified the UNCRC on 3 August 1990 (with reservations consistent with Islamic Sharia) — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 1991 Muslim Personal Status Law substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Muslim Personal Status Law 1991
key_developments.description: Federal Muslim Personal Status Law enacted codifying Hanafi personal-status framework. Arts. 110-134 govern hadana (custody) and wilaya (guardianship). Substantively significant Islamic-law-codification framework persisting through subsequent regime-changes.

key_developments.title: ICC arrest warrant for Bashir + War-in-Darfur framework
key_developments.description: International Criminal Court arrest warrant for Omar al-Bashir 4 March 2009 — substantively distinctive globally first-ICC-arrest-warrant-for-sitting-head-of-state framework. War in Darfur 2003-2020 substantively distinctive Northeast African armed-conflict framework with estimated 300,000 killed substantially affecting demographic and family-law-implementation framework.

key_developments.title: Post-Bashir transitional government + Sudanese Revolution framework
key_developments.description: Bashir government overthrown 11 April 2019 following Sudanese Revolution (December 2018-April 2019) — substantively distinctive Northeast African pro-democracy-protest framework. Transitional government signalled (partial) reform of Sharia-based provisions affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Sudan Civil War 2023-onwards + SAF-RSF conflict framework
key_developments.description: Sudan Civil War 15 April 2023-onwards between Sudanese Armed Forces (Burhan) and Rapid Support Forces (Hemedti) — substantively distinctive globally largest-displacement-crisis-currently framework with estimated 150,000+ killed and 12+ million displaced through 2026 substantially affecting demographic and family-law-implementation framework. Substantive cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework affecting South-Sudan and Chad refugee-flows.

key_developments.title: Supreme Court + Personal Status Law operational suspension + UN-OCHA refugee-family-law framework
key_developments.description: Supreme Court of Sudan operational framework substantially affected by ongoing civil war 2023-2024 — Personal Status Law for Muslims 1991 + Personal Status Law for Non-Muslims 2003 (Christians) substantive operational suspension in conflict-affected areas. UN-OCHA + UNHCR coordination framework substantively significant for refugee-family-law adjudication including custody disputes in Chad/Egypt/South-Sudan refugee-host contexts. Substantive Hanafi-Sharia framework continuing in non-conflict areas without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Sudan operates a Hanafi-Sunni Sharia religious-law federal framework with parallel non-Muslim personal-status framework — places Sudan in the African Hanafi Sharia cluster.
Pre-1983 secular framework + 1983 Sharia reform + post-2019 partial reform efforts represent structurally distinctive religious-law trajectory.
Personal Status Law for Non-Muslims 1926 is among the earliest colonial-era African personal-status statutes within the corpus.
Non-Hague Convention status places Sudan in the non-Hague Northeast African cluster.
editorial_notes: Sudan jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Anglo-Egyptian-to-2023-Civil-War trajectory: 1926-Personal-Status-Law-for-Non-Muslims-+-Anglo-Egyptian + 1956-Sudan-independence-+-Republic + 1969-Nimeiri-coup-+-16-year-authoritarian + 1983-September-Laws-+-Sharia-reform + 1989-Bashir-coup-+-NIF-+-30-year-Islamist-regime + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 1991-Muslim-Personal-Status-Law + 2009-ICC-arrest-warrant-+-War-in-Darfur + 2019-Post-Bashir-transitional-+-Sudanese-Revolution + 2023-Sudan-Civil-War-+-SAF-RSF-conflict.
Religious-law Hanafi-Sunni Northeast Africa (Muslim Personal Status Law 1991 + Personal Status Law for Non-Muslims 1926 + non-Hague). 1983 Sharia reform + 2019 transitional reform trajectory.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive family-law analysis under Muslim Personal Status Law 1991 arts. 110-134 + Personal Status Law for Non-Muslims 1926 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Northeast-African + religious-law-Hanafi-Sunni + parallel-non-Muslim-framework + September-Laws-1983-trigger-Second-Sudanese-Civil-War-1983-2005 + Nimeiri-16-year-authoritarian-1969-1985 + Bashir-30-year-Islamist-regime-1989-2019 + ICC-arrest-warrant-Bashir-2009-first-ICC-arrest-warrant-for-sitting-head-of-state-globally-distinctive + War-in-Darfur-2003-2020-300000-killed + Sudanese-Revolution-2018-2019 + Sudan-Civil-War-2023-onwards-SAF-RSF-Burhan-Hemedti-+-largest-displacement-crisis-currently-globally-150000-killed-12-million-displaced + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
